title: Hyundai Motor Group Opens ZER01NE Fund III to Drive Future Technology Innovation with Startups
content: Hyundai Motor Group has unveiled ZER01NE Fund III, a KRW 125 billion (~USD 91M) strategic investment initiative aimed at nurturing early-stage startups in next-gen technologies. With support from 10 Group affiliates, this third fund represents a significant scale-up from its predecessors and reflects Hyundaiâ€™s sharpened focus on hydrogen and energy technologies as central pillars of its sustainability strategy. The fund will invest in startups globally that align with the Groupâ€™s vision for decarbonization and energy transition. Hydrogen, in particular, is a priority area as Hyundai continues to lead in fuel cell innovation and hydrogen mobility solutions. Through this fund, the Group aims to foster partnerships with startups working on breakthroughs in hydrogen production, storage, infrastructure, and utilization. Previous funds have supported companies advancing sustainable technologies such as LD Carbon (tire recycling), Terracle (plastic upcycling), and 60Hertz (distributed renewable energy systems). ZER01NE Fund III builds on that foundation, seeking deeper synergy with hydrogen innovators to accelerate Hyundaiâ€™s clean energy roadmap.
author: Analysts Account
